title: Wheel Encoder Odometry
content: Quadrature encoders attached to drive wheels count pulses to measure wheel rotation.  Distance per pulse: Δd = π · D_wheel / CPR, where D_wheel is wheel diameter and CPR is counts per revolution.
extended_content: Quadrature encoding uses two phase-offset signals (A and B) to determine both direction and count, giving 4× resolution per physical slot (4·CPR effective resolution).  Typical robot-vacuum encoder: 600 CPR magnetic or optical; effective resolution ≈ 0.1 mm per count for a 75 mm wheel.  Slip detection: if commanded motor speed and encoder-measured speed diverge beyond a threshold, the robot slows down or triggers a stuck condition.  Cumulative odometry error typically 1–3% of path length.
source: Borenstein, Everett & Feng, Navigating Mobile Robots, A.K. Peters, 1996
